here's the thing though before this turns into a big week-long controversy: if you regularly have intense workloads, you're not buying a MacBook Air. https://twitter.com/...

Celsius claims they may have liabilities up to $10B, but their top creditors are owed less than $200m. The only conclusion is that Mashinsky masterminded what probably is the biggest retail rug in crypto history. A shadow bank pretending not to be a bank https://www.bloomberg.com/...
And it's over. I hope people get some of their money back. What a disaster. https://www.cnbc.com/...

Yet another crypto collapse. Which reminds me, imho #NFTs are a scam. I hate the entire concept. I've seen some great Aztec themed artwork on this site that I'd love to retweet, but because they're into this grift, I boycott it. #SorryNotSorry https://twitter.com/...

This would be the right move. The bill is in no fit state to become law. If I'm elected Prime Minister I will ensure the bill doesn't overreach. We should not be legislating for hurt feelings. https://twitter.com/...

Hearing that the Online Safety Bill has been dropped from government business next week with a view for it to “come back in the autumn” (aka when there's a new PM Read: it's being dropped Pretty key piece of govt legislation gone
🚨 Understand the Online Safety Bill has been dropped off the draft version of government business for next week. They say it will be “back in the autumn”. But will it really? 🤔

I'm appalled that Tory MP and candidate for Prime Minister @KemiBadenoch describes the results of online bullying, harassment, and vile targeted abuse of children and adults which results in trauma, depression, and suicide as “hurt feelings”. Disgraceful. https://twitter.com/...
Which part of the bill legislates for hurt feelings, Kemi? https://twitter.com/...

Nobody would say the Online Harms Bill has been rushed. It's bounced around DCMS for years. And if it's so important, as he implies, why so vague about “if” it returns? Contradictory: “I look forward to this essential bill, if it returns.” https://twitter.com/...
It is not impossible the Culture Secretary doesn't actually know what's in the Online Safety Bill. https://twitter.com/...

Government's Flagship Online Safety Bill Set To Be Dropped From Commons Business. It may just be temporary but we now have more time to fight this dangerous attack on our freedom of speech online. https://www.politicshome.com/ ...

The Online Safety Bill is/was a classic eg of a big Johnson-era failing - never imagining Labour getting into gov. 'Yes, let's give Whitehall power to dictate what people can say online, and every aspect of how every school teaches. That could never be turned against US!'
The plan reportedly still is for it to be tabled in the autumn A DCMS source has insisted it is not true the bill is being dropped entirely

If - *if* - the #OnlineSafetyBill has gone away and it is not just a temporary calendaring hiatus (as it might well be), I imagine the pressure on the sketchy Part 3 #DEAct judicial review will increase. People got product to shift.

Online Safety Bill dropped from Commons business This is huge They say ‘to come back in autumn’. But we should keep letting them know how bad it is for a free society Keep making voices heard #together https://www.politicshome.com/ ...

I understand the Bill was dropped next week to make room for the Labour motion of no confidence A govt source says: “Labour reduced the amount of time for genuine parliamentary business so they could play politics. They are reaping what they sowed.”
Great news if so. This awful Bill would have UK sleepwalking into a censorship regime https://www.google.com/... https://twitter.com/...

We need to ensure Online Safety Bill is kicked into touch #together It's an abhorrent attack on the core of our Society: without free speech & open debate we have no freedom Outsourcing censorship to Big Tech is cowardly dangerous & wrong. Stay vigilant on this https://twitter.com/...
Huge if true... and a phenomenal success for those of us who have fought it absolutely tirelessly. I'm not at all complacent though. Delayed doesn't mean ditched! https://twitter.com/...
Source says it was between the OSB and NI Protocol legislation - the latter won out

Worth pointing out: some of the passages of the online safety bill that have been flagged as banning “hurt feelings” are in fact a *softening* of laws already on the books in the UK, where the Malicious Communications Act makes it illegal to be rude online https://www.theguardian.com/ ...

It's been a great pleasure to help Tesla towards its goals over the last 5 years and a difficult decision to part ways. In that time, Autopilot graduated from lane keeping to city streets and I look forward to seeing the exceptionally strong Autopilot team continue that momentum.
@karpathy Thanks for everything you have done for Tesla! It has been an honor working with you.
I have no concrete plans for what's next but look to spend more time revisiting my long-term passions around technical work in AI, open source and education.
Tesla's AI program is now driverless. https://twitter.com/...
Looks like the forced return to office is leading to more senior casualties than expected? 🙃 https://twitter.com/...

Departure of Karpathy is significant. He has been vocal proponent and defender of Autopilot in last 6-12 months. He's leaving when 8 people have died in 6 Tesla crashes in the last 2 months - all w hallmarks of Autopilot involvement (high speed, no braking/evasive steering) 1/n https://twitter.com/...

Karpathy said it best. From the time he started Tesla to today they've gone from basic lane keeping to full city streets driving. Nobody can deny that the difference between the software when he started and FSD Beta today is just night and day. And it all runs on the same cars. https://twitter.com/...
Andrej Karpathy was the Director of AI. he lead the AI team. Ashok leads Autopilot Software, which is the larger team with the engineers who actually make the Autopilot updates. @elonmusk

Andrej Karpathy's transition out of Tesla has been planned for a long time. Starting over a year ago Elon started making a point to everyone that Ashok was leading Autopilot software, not Andrej. Now I know that it's because Andrej was starting to talk about moving on.
Elon is a talent magnet, they said. People will be lining up to work for him at Twitter, they said https://twitter.com/...

Yesterday I was looking at Elon's Twitter clownshow and wondering how Elon managed to hang onto onto Andrej when previously being head of autonomy at Tesla was like being Spinal Tap's drummer. And today... https://twitter.com/...

New: Happy Prime Day! If you're thinking about buying a Ring doorbell, you should know Amazon gave doorbell footage to police without owner consent 11 times this year https://www.politico.com/...

If you get any home surveillance cameras, either get ones that don't store in the cloud or use those that use Apple's HomeKit secure video (which is end-to-end encrypted, so Apple doesn't have access to your videos). https://twitter.com/...

I asked Ring if it would notify users in the event footage was shared w/o consent numerous times over about a year. Usually, officials would behave like they never saw the question, skipping over it while answering others, then not responding when I repeated myself. https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

New: Ring, the Amazon-owned video doorbell maker with thousands of cozy partnerships with police departments, gave a record amount of doorbell footage and other user information to U.S. authorities in 2021, according to its latest figures. https://techcrunch.com/...
11 seems like a VAST undercounting https://twitter.com/...

Amazon's Ring products have made it more difficult to exist in public without being recorded. Ring revealed it provided law enforcement with user footage through a process not requiring user consent 11 times already this year. We cannot accept this surveillance as inevitable. https://twitter.com/...

Ring says police can't view recordings unless they are posted publicly or shared directly with police. But this doesn't apply to emergency requests or subpoenas. In 2022, Amazon shared footage with police 11 times without owner consent. @Politico reports: https://www.politico.com/...

Amazon has given police surveillance footage from Ring cameras without asking the owner's permission *or* obtaining a warrant 11 times this year alone, according to a new letter sent to Sen. Ed Markey https://theintercept.com/...

Amazon doesn't just want to take your money, they want to take your privacy and civil liberties. Giving your Ring camera footage to police without a warrant or consent is unacceptable. https://theintercept.com/...

We're thrilled Netflix has selected Microsoft as its advertising technology and sales partner. We want publishers to have more long-term viable ad monetization platforms, so more people can access the content they love wherever they are. https://blogs.microsoft.com/ ...
Unlike Google (YouTube) and Comcast (Peacock), who were other potential advertising partners, Microsoft doesn't own/operate a competing streaming service with Netflix. https://www.cnbc.com/...

“More importantly, Microsoft offered the flexibility to innovate over time on both the technology and sales side” “Microsoft said all ads served on Netflix will be available exclusively through Microsoft” https://www.wsj.com/...

Given that it's 1. least-discussed outcome of $MSFT AND 2. Greg Peters (who oversees games, too) making the announcement 3. I think there's a lot more to this partnership. https://about.netflix.com/...

This is the streaming version of Microsoft being the provider of ads for Yahoo! Search. It makes total sense for Netflix to outsource this versus try to build from scratch especially as time is of the essence. Helps that Microsoft isn't a competitor. https://www.theverge.com/...

After years of stating that commercials would never be on Netflix, Netflix chooses Microsoft as its advertising partner. Cheaper ad version of Netflix supposedly coming later this year https://twitter.com/...
Exciting news from @Microsoft and @Netflix today! We're thrilled to be part of this exclusive partnership powering Netflix's first ad-supported subscription plan. https://blogs.microsoft.com/ ...
season 5 of STRANGER THINGS: it's the mid '00s, the kids are all grown up, and we realize we're in the alternate timeline where zunes won https://twitter.com/...
Microsoft wins the Netflix ad biz! https://twitter.com/...

An in-depth look at the rise and fall of crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital, whose founders Su Zhu and Kyle Davies bet everything on prices only going up

Really good article. Reminds me of the Chuck Prince quote: “When the music stops, in terms of liquidity, things will be complicated. But as long as the music is playing, you've got to get up and dance.” https://twitter.com/...

Think this @hosseeb quote really captures what most struck me writing about 3AC They really practiced what they preached! They really bought the dip, even on margin! In May, when Luna was collapsing, Kyle was still talking about buying BTC and ETH *on margin* https://twitter.com/... https://twitter.com/...

Three Arrows was a venture investor in some of the best-known crypto startups and, in some cases, manager of their corporate treasuries. It was both an aggressive borrower from large lenders and a shareholder in some of those lenders https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Three Arrows became the most important hedge fund in crypto. But when the price of Bitcoin fell, the fund's sudden collapse revealed how interconnected the crypto industry is https://www.bloomberg.com/...

I learned recently that the phrase “hedge fund” applies to a specific shape of legal entity and does not require that the entity have any actual hedging strategy, which is how you get a company that works only if crypto keeps going up forever. https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Some Crypto trading platforms have since disclosed exposures to 3AC. One has gone under after 3AC defaulted on a loan worth more than $650 million. Its customers have had their crypto accounts frozen and are unlikely to get all their assets back https://www.bloomberg.com/...
At least when Lehman went bankrupt, people knew where to find them: https://www.cnbc.com/...
GREAT READ: Three Arrows bet everything on Bitcoin's rise. When prices fell, the hedge fund's sudden collapse rocked the industry https://www.bloomberg.com/... via @BW
Tale as old as time: “What surprises me most about 3AC's collapse was how they were able to amass so much leverage in the first place” https://www.bloomberg.com/... via @BW

“They might have been stupid and misguided .. But they really believe this stuff, and you can see it in their books, right? You wouldn't have traded this way, if you didn't believe that this was true.” #crypto ⁦@business⁩ https://www.bloomberg.com/...

Wrote about the rise and fall of Three Arrows Capital with @MuyaoShen and @BenBartenstein Between their big bank trading experience and crypto connections, everyone assumed they were pros. “They turned out to be degens like everyone else.” https://www.bloomberg.com/... @crypto @BW

FINALLY!! If you wonder how exactly 3AC turned to crypto's Archegos or Long-Term Capital Management, we investigated and reported from Singapore, Dubai, to London, and New York. w @justinaknope @BenBartenstein via @crypto @BW https://www.bloomberg.com/...

How Crypto Hedge Fund Three Arrows Capital Fell Apart (3AC) - Bloomberg “Su Zhu and Kyle Davies turned 3AC into the most important hedge fund in crypto, but they bet everything on prices only going up.” That is not a hedge fun, it was a gambling operation https://www.bloomberg.com/...
Meta releases its first human rights report, including due diligence in 2020 and 2021 and a summary of a controversial human rights impact assessment of India
Facebook Parent Meta Details Human-Rights Efforts
A Closer Look: Meta's First Annual Human Rights Report

It's not often I get to be part of a transformative moment, but today we released Meta's First Annual Human Rights Report. In this report, @Meta is taking a major step to uphold human rights principles. We still have much work to do, but it's been an honor to work on this. https://twitter.com/...

New: Meta said last year they'd start releasing annual reports on how its services affect human rights globally. The first is out. They detail efforts to combat human trafficking, misinfo, attacks on election integrity and more. Story with @sal19: https://www.wsj.com/...

Meta strives to apply human rights principles to every facet of our business. We are proud of the progress documented in this report and recognize there's more work to be done. https://about.fb.com/... https://twitter.com/...

This report shares a lot of the amazing work of the human rights team at Meta. It includes lot of detailed insights and actions from Meta's human rights due diligence on products, countries and responses to emerging crises. https://about.fb.com/...

Today we're releasing @Meta's first annual human rights report, an important step forward on our human rights journey and a demonstration of our commitment to “know and show” our human rights impact, as the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights expect of us. 1/x

Today's report represents a detailed look at the impact of that work - from fighting harmful misinformation and ensuring AI governance is rooted in democratic values - and the steps we're taking to learn, improve and hold ourselves accountable. https://about.fb.com/...

BREAKING: Meta/Facebook releases long awaited India Human Rights Impact Assessment(HRIA) report commissioned in 2019. four page summary of report doesn't tell much about the findings & recommends by independent experts who conducted assessment Link: https://about.fb.com/... https://twitter.com/...

China's crackdown and economic downturn have led to massive layoffs in its tech sector, but some workers are relieved to exit the industry's “996” work schedule

We're thrilled to announce that we have entered into an agreement to merge with ironSource to accelerate a unique end-to-end platform that powers creators' success! Learn more on our blog. https://blog.unity.com/...

Unity today announced that it will merge with ironSource (although Unity shareholders will own ~3/4 of the company). While surprising as a stand-alone deal, this merger is emblematic of consolidation taking place across mobile. Thoughts in a thread (1/X) https://mobiledevmemo.com/...

Hello ⁦@Apple⁩ Car Lead! 🙌 🤩 Longtime @Tesla executive who played an important role in developing the company's advanced driver-assistance system known as #Autopilot is leaving the company, in the electric-vehicle maker's latest exec departure. 🫠 https://www.wsj.com/...

4/ So why merge? I offer two reasons: Monetization (esp. Mediation) and Profit (a few people suggested that I should have swapped the order of these). Mediation is the means by which critical pricing data can be collected absent the IDFA-indexed feedback loop post-ATT. https://twitter.com/...

5/ And iS is a profitable company, whereas Unity has never been profitable. Unity claimed it could be profitable this year in its Q1 earnings call; the combined company believes it can achieve $1BN in Adj EBITDA run rate by end of 2024. https://twitter.com/...

7/ In a fragmented market I doubt either company could have commanded more than low double-digit SSP / mediation market share; in combination, assuming complementary functionality between iS' various advertiser-facing tools, potentially real competition comes to this market. https://twitter.com/...

6/ My sense is that this merger was motivated by Applovin's acquisition of MoPub, which gives it a plurality if not majority of in-game advertising supply now. Aggregation of supply is critical in producing data needed to optimize advertiser campaigns https://mobiledevmemo.com/...

3/ Both companies have seen their share prices pummeled since ATT was introduced ($U down 61%, $IS down 80%). These companies are also suffering from the general correction in high-growth tech stocks. ATT isnt the sole factor but it certainly has created headwinds. https://twitter.com/...

Unity and ironSource confirmed that they are merging of “two powerhouses” in interactive development and app monetization, valuing @ironSource at $4.4B. @ingridlunden has the story here: https://techcrunch.com/...

2/ First, why? ATT created a more challenging operating environment for mobile advertising. Unity and iS are among the largest mobile gaming advertising networks, but iS has a mature & scaled mediation platform. https://mobiledevmemo.com/...
